subject: Enable CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF (#27)
author: fjmartinez2k

upstream.vanilla_quote:
config PANTHERLORD_FF
	bool "Pantherlord force feedback support"
	depends on HID_PANTHERLORD
	select INPUT_FF_MEMLESS

provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md (grouped row with 10 other defconfig commits)

impact.effect_if_absent: Pantherlord and GreenAsia game controllers would not have force feedback (rumble/vibration) support

notes: Grouped defconfig entry in patch-provenance.md (11 commits total). Commit enabled CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF in fork's arch/arm/configs/MiSTer_defconfig, changing it from disabled (# CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF is not set) to enabled (CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF=y). The option exists in vanilla 6.18 (drivers/hid/Kconfig:896) and is already enabled in our 6.18 config (board/mister/de10nano/linux.config). Stock 5.15 image (IKCONFIG snapshot) also has CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF=y. Not carried as a patch; the configuration change has been adopted into our linux.config. Userspace coupling confirmed: Main_MiSTer uses FF_RUMBLE ioctl for rumble effect support across multiple device types including Pantherlord controllers. No conflicts or regressions detected. | sonnet-verified: DEVICE-ID CORRECTION. Re-derived drivers/hid/hid-pl.c's pl_devices[] table directly (this is the actual CONFIG_PANTHERLORD_FF-gated driver, compiled as hid-pl.o per drivers/hid/Makefile:100 'obj-$(CONFIG_HID_PANTHERLORD) += hid-pl.o'): it matches {USB_VENDOR_ID_GAMERON(0x0810), USB_DEVICE_ID_GAMERON_DUAL_PSX_ADAPTOR(0x0001)}, {GAMERON, DUAL_PCS_ADAPTOR(0x0002)}, {USB_VENDOR_ID_GREENASIA(0x0e8f), 0x0003}, {USB_VENDOR_ID_JESS2(0x0f30), JESS2_COLOR_RUMBLE_PAD(0x0111)} (drivers/hid/hid-ids.h:537-539,581-583,749-750). Note: GAMERON and PANTHERLORD are two separate #define names in hid-ids.h that happen to share the literal vendor ID 0x0810 (PANTHERLORD_TWIN_USB_JOYSTICK is also 0x0001, hid-quirks.c:160) — same rebranded hardware, so labeling 0810:0001/0002 'Pantherlord' is defensible. REMOVED '1209:FACA / ControllaBLE Bluetooth gamepad' — exhaustive grep of drivers/hid/*.c and hid-ids.h for '0x1209', '0xFACA', and 'ControllaBLE' found zero matches; this device ID does not appear anywhere in the pl_devices[] table or the kernel tree and was hallucinated in the original pass. userspace_coupling citations independently re-verified byte-exact: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:4881 is literally 'fef->type = FF_RUMBLE;' and :5228 is literally 'if (test_bit(FF_RUMBLE, ff_features)) {' — both confirmed correct, no change needed. Disposition dropped-deliberate was already correct (config enable IS present in our linux.config, matches the adopted/superseded_by pattern) — no disposition change, device_ids/affected_hardware arrays corrected only.
